extraction.one_line_summary: HIGUITA Sergio attacked long-range before final climb, held solo to summit finish while LOPEZ distanced QUINTANA/POGACAR late to reclaim white jersey.
tactical_patterns: HIGUITA mid-season transfer signing (joined May 1) executed winning long-range move suggesting EF's talent identification outperformed traditional scouting.
LOPEZ made three successive attacks on final climb, demonstrating calculated aggression prioritizing white jersey defense over stage hunting.
MOVISTAR rode defensively for QUINTANA despite holding 2nd GC, allowing LOPEZ to gain 59 seconds and drop QUINTANA to 3rd.
ROGLIC isolated early but defended red by sitting wheels rather than controlling, revealing confidence in time buffer over active defense.

rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: Third-division rider in April winning stage 18 of Grand Tour indicates exceptional climbing engine missed by major teams.
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: Triple-attacking final climb while managing white jersey math shows tactical maturity unusual for fourth-place GC rider.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Dropped on final climb after moving to 2nd GC previous day, suggests vulnerability when isolated without MOVISTAR numbers.
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: Lost 59 seconds and white jersey on stage 18 despite being 20 years old, rare crack in debut Grand Tour.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Defended red with minimal teammates by conservation rather than control, banking on 2m24s buffer holding through mountains.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Sprinted for time bonuses at finish despite being 3rd GC, showing opportunistic racing uncommon in veteran domestique role.
